prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7661) and high seasonality variance (0.9051), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates trend tracking while FT_catboost excels at seasonal capture, suggesting complementary strengths. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, but given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "THETA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ETS", "THETA", "FT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveSeasonal", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ARIMA", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7661,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7661) and high seasonality variance (0.9051), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates trend tracking while FT_catboost excels at seasonal capture, suggesting complementary strengths. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, but given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates high model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.1065 shows moderate disagreement but seasonality_corr_variance=0.9051 reveals models capture patterns very differently. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high variance scenarios. With n_windows=3 (very few), shrinkage≥0.3 is needed to prevent overfitting. DBA handles phase-shifted peaks, best_per_horizon captures horizon specialists, inverse_rmse_weights provides RMSE-based weighting with shrinkage, topk_mean adds ensemble diversity, and exp_weighted_average adapts to recent performance."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has 5 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All use anti-leakage methods (selection strategies use rolling/expanding selection from past windows only; baselines never leak). Universe leaderboard top candidates are represented or similar variants exist (topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 is similar to k3, inverse_rmse_weights_k4 variants are similar to k3). No redundancy issues (no type has ≥4 candidates). n_windows=3 ≥ 3, weighted candidates present. Score margin between top2 is small (0.012) but acceptable.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k4"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 which has best MAPE (0.06647) and SMAPE (0.06715) in universe leaderboards. Current set already has good diversity with selection, weighted, and dba methods. n_windows=3 is low so keeping k values conservative (k3/k4). relative_spread_mean=0.106 < 0.25 so disagreement is moderate - no need for aggressive trimming.", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 for better MAPE coverage"], "when_good": "When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods as they become viable per academic knowledge."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7661) and high seasonality variance (0.9051), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_rf dominates trend tracking while FT_catboost excels at seasonal capture, suggesting complementary strengths. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, but given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.1065 shows moderate disagreement but seasonality_corr_variance=0.9051 reveals models capture patterns very differently. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high variance scenarios. With n_windows=3 (very few), shrinkage≥0.3 is needed to prevent overfitting. DBA handles phase-shifted peaks, best_per_horizon captures horizon specialists, inverse_rmse_weights provides RMSE-based weighting with shrinkage, topk_mean adds ensemble diversity, and exp_weighted_average adapts to recent performance.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has 5 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All use anti-leakage methods (selection strategies use rolling/expanding selection from past windows only; baselines never leak). Universe leaderboard top candidates are represented or similar variants exist (topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 is similar to k3, inverse_rmse_weights_k4 variants are similar to k3). No redundancy issues (no type has ≥4 candidates). n_windows=3 ≥ 3, weighted candidates present. Score margin between top2 is small (0.012) but acceptable.

statistician.rationale: Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 which has best MAPE (0.06647) and SMAPE (0.06715) in universe leaderboards. Current set already has good diversity with selection, weighted, and dba methods. n_windows=3 is low so keeping k values conservative (k3/k4). relative_spread_mean=0.106 < 0.25 so disagreement is moderate - no need for aggressive trimming.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 for better MAPE coverage
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods as they become viable per academic knowledge.
